title: pkcs11 put: create-before-destroy ordering is the correct safety choice
description: The ordering (collect existing handles, create new object, destroy old handles) ensures that if create_object fails, the existing secret is unmodified. The previous ordering (destroy then create) had a window with no valid object. A contributor who wants to 'simplify to destroy-then-create' should be told: during the destroy-to-create window, a concurrent get() returns NotFound. The atomicity comment documents this correctly. The create-before-destroy choice is correct and defensible.
